LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT RBZ LLP'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TRANSFERRING VENUE TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT VENUE IS IMPROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO. VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO. SEE CCP 395.5. FOR EXAMPLE, THE CONTRACTS BETWEEN THE PARTIES WERE ENTERED INTO IN SAN FRANCISCO DURING THE RELEVANT PERIOD, WHERE IT WAS REVIEWED AND SIGNED BY MR. CURIALE FOR THE FIRM. (CURIALE DECL. 3 AND 5.) THE ALLEGED INJURIES TO PLAINTIFF OCCURRED IN SAN FRANCISCO, WHERE HE IS LOCATED. THE REQUESTS FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING THAT SHE PRACTICED LAW AT WHAT WAS THEN KNOWN AS HOWARD RICE FROM DEC. 1981 TO MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992-1995. (JUDGE MILLER SUBSEQUENTLY MOVED ON TO OTHER POSITIONS BEFORE SHE WAS APPOINTED TO THE BENCH IN FEBRUARY 2005.) HOWARD RICE MERGED INTO ARNOLD & PORTER. JERRY FALK, ONE OF JUDGE MILLER'S FORMER PARTNERS AT HOWARD RICE, RETIRED EARLIER THIS YEAR. IN JANUARY 2013, JUDGE MILLER WAS GUEST AT A RETIREMENT DINNER FOR MR. FALK THAT WAS HOSTED BY WHAT IS NOW ARNOLD & PORTER, TO WHICH MAY CURRENT AND FORMER COLLEAGUES OF MR. FALK'S FROM THE ORDER HOWARD RICE FIRM WERE INVITED. THERE WERE ABOUT 100 PEOPLE AT THE DINNER. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. JUDGE MILLER DOES NOT BELIEVE ANYONE AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD BELIEVE THAT THE COURT COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. JUDGE MILLER DOES NOT KNOW JONATHAN HUGHES OR THE OTHER LAWYER LISTED ON THE PLEADINGS FOR RBZ. JUDGE MILLER GAVE COUNSEL TO THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFF RICHARD CURIALE'S MOTION TO COMPEL STEPHEN HIRSCHFELD, JOHN BAUM, CARMEN PLAZA DE JENNINGS AND GLENN KRAEMER TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY IS DENIED. SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS. JUDGE MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES, NOT REPORTED. = (302/PTJ)

Off Calendar Nov-21-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Set For Trial On Aug-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Stephen Hirschfeld, John Baum, Carmen Plaza De Jennings And Glenn Kraemer To Produce Documents
Off Calendar Aug-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chris Keller, Esq. of Curiale Wilson LLP, Tel. 415.794.5308, for plaintiff and moving party;
Jon Hughes, Esq. of Arnold & Porter, Tel. 415.471.3156, for defendant and opposing party;
Gerrett M. Fahy, Esq. of Chapman Glucksman Dean Roeb & Barger, Tel. 310.207.7722, for defendant RBZ, LLP.

Judge Miller disclosed at the hearing that she practiced law at what was then known as Howard Rice from Dec. 1981 to March 1987, and from 1992-1995. (Judge Miller subsequently moved on to other positions before she was appointed to the bench in February 2005.) Howard Rice merged into Arnold & Porter. Jerry Falk, one of Judge Miller's former partners at Howard Rice, retired earlier this year. In January 2013, Judge Miller was guest at a retirement dinner for Mr. Falk that was hosted by what is now Arnold & Porter, to which may current and former colleagues of Mr. Falk's from the order Howard Rice firm were invited. There were about 100 people at the dinner. This will not affect the Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Judge Miller does not believe anyone aware of these facts would believe that the Court could not be fair and impartial. Judge Miller does not know Jonathan Hughes or the other lawyer listed on the pleadings for RBZ. Judge Miller gave counsel to the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled on PLAINTIFF RICHARD CURIALE'S Motion To Compel Stephen Hirschfeld, John Baum, Carmen Plaza De Jennings And Glenn Kraemer To Produce Documents as follows: Motion to compel discovery is denied. Counsel for plaintiff and counsel for subpoenaed individuals ("Hirschfeld partners") have reached agreement on requests 7, 8, 22 and 23. Counsel argued re subpoena requests 1-6 and 9-14. As to requests 1-6, no further response is required. Hirschfeld partners have produced responsive documents up to the date of the subpoenas. As to request 9-14, Hirschfeld partners have produced responsive documents up to the time in September 2012 when Plaintiff left the law firm. The motion to compel production of additional documents pursuant to request 9-14, including through the date of the subpoena, is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that further documents comprised of internal communications among Hirschfeld law firm are legally relevant. Even if they were, this additional production would pose an unreasonable burden on non-parties, particularly where there is no showing that these documents cannot be obtained from defendant RBZ, LLP. The request for sanctions is denied. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2013 Text Ruling

Ntc Of Motion And Motion For An Order Transferring Venue To Los Angeles County
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Garrett M. Fahy, Esq. of Chapman Glucksman Dean Roeb & Barger, for defendant and moving party;
Chris Keller, Esq. of Curiale Wilson, Tel. 415.794.5308, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RBZ LLP's Motion For An Order Transferring Venue To Los Angeles County is denied in its entirety. Defendant has failed to meet its burden to show that venue is improper in San Francisco. Venue is proper in San Francisco. See CCP 395.5. For example, the contracts between the parties were entered into in San Francisco during the relevant period, where it was reviewed and signed by Mr. Curiale for the firm. (Curiale Decl. 3 and 5.) The alleged injuries to Plaintiff occurred in San Francisco, where he is located. The requests for attorneys fees and costs are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
